what_it_does: Renders a modal overlay work-queue ("What needs you") that lists pending stack items from a shared decisions store, dispatches each row by its `item.type`, and opens a selected decision by firing a window `decision:open` custom event so the shared decision host renders the card.
observations: Only one concrete stack-item type is handled: `decision-sequence`
Unknown item types are rendered as an explicit, non-interactive 'view not ready' row instead of being dropped or crashing
The overlay closes via Escape key, scrim click, and a close button
A selected decision is opened through the same event path as a deep link, passing `fromInbox: true`
Initial load errors show a red banner with retry; refresh errors show a quiet note while the last-good list remains visible
Copy is described as an AI-supplied draft, plain-language, pending final ratification by Tim
The component is an App-root sibling overlay with ARIA dialog attributes
standards: The fail-loud default in `renderStackItem` makes unlanded item types visible rather than silent
The single `decision:open` event path avoids duplicating decision-card render logic inside the inbox
Refresh-error handling distinguishes 'could not load at all' from 'could not refresh the cached list'
conventions: React functional component with `useEffect` for keyboard handling
TypeScript types imported from a sibling store module
CSS class names follow a BEM-ish `inbox-*` namespace
Accessibility attributes (`role`, `aria-label`, `aria-modal`, `aria-hidden`) on the overlay and controls
concerns: The Escape key listener is attached to `window`, which could interact with other open modals if not coordinated
No memoization of the mapped item list or row callbacks
The list is closed before the `decision:open` event is dispatched, so an event-handling failure leaves the inbox closed
The `pick` callback signature is `(address, id)` but is constructed inline and passed through `renderStackItem`
notes: A modal operator work-queue that surfaces pending decisions honestly, dispatches rows by type with a safe default, and hands off to the shared decision card via a single custom event.
questions: What component listens for the `decision:open` event and renders the decision card?
What other `StackItem` types are planned for the A4 item-type registry?
How do `error` and `refreshError` differ in the store implementation?
What fields does `StackItem` expose for non-decision item types?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file implements the described channel-stack view, single open path, and typed-dispatch seam with a fail-loud default.
apparent_themes: Fail-loud: no silent drops for unknown data
Single source of truth for opening decisions
Extensible composition seam for item-type renderers
intention_signals: Build a centralized, trustworthy operator work-queue that can surface any autonomous work requiring human settlement, with clear handling for partial data, errors, and future item types.
novelty: The explicit 'view not ready' placeholder row for unknown stack-item types, and the quiet refresh-error note that keeps the last-good list usable.
contribution: A reusable modal overlay plus a typed item dispatch that surfaces pending decisions and future item types without losing them, and an event-based handoff to the shared decision-card host.
summary_for_embedding: DecisionsInbox.tsx is a React modal overlay that renders the operator's pending 'what needs you' stack from a shared store, dispatches rows by item type with a fail-loud default, and opens decision cards via a window custom event.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a single, extensible work-queue view where the operator can see and enter any surfaced item requiring human settlement.

symbols.does: Exported React component that renders the 'What needs you' modal overlay and its pending-item list.
symbols.description: It reads the shared decisions store, conditionally shows loading, error, empty, and refresh-error states, wires Escape/scrim/close-button dismissal, and renders each pending stack item through `renderStackItem`.

symbols.does: Local keydown handler that closes the inbox when Escape is pressed while the overlay is open.
symbols.description: Attached to `window` inside a `useEffect` gated by the `open` flag; removes itself on cleanup.

symbols.does: Callback that closes the inbox and fires a `decision:open` custom event for the selected item.
symbols.description: Passes the item's `address`, `id`, and `fromInbox: true` so the shared decision host opens the card and can return to the inbox afterward.

symbols.does: Typed dispatch function that chooses a renderer based on `item.type`.
symbols.description: Returns a `DecisionRow` for `decision-sequence`; for any unknown type it renders an honest, visible placeholder row so the item is never silently dropped.

symbols.does: Component that renders a single decision stack item as a clickable preview button.
symbols.description: Displays the item's name, optional meaning, recommended label, and reversibility note, letting the operator assess stakes before tapping to open the full decision card.
